region_of_origin: France / United Kingdom (widely adopted in Singapore)

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic tart class; no specific lemon meringue tart entry exists. Closest parent match applied.
ontology.foodex2_description: Tarts and flans — sweet pastry with filling
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC covers sweet tarts and flans broadly; no lemon meringue tart-specific code exists. Closest parent match applied.

main_ingredients: shortcrust pastry (wheat flour, butter, sugar, egg)
lemon curd (lemon juice, lemon zest, eggs, butter, sugar)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard lemon meringue tart piece as sold in Singapore bakeries and cafes, typically weighing approximately 85 g.

nutrient_source.title: Lemon Meringue Tart — Singapore Health Promotion Board Foodo Database (G00010483)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: All per_100g macros and micronutrients are taken directly from the HPB Foodo record G00010483 for Lemon Meringue Tart X 1Pc. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference 45226617 (lemon meringue tart, commercially prepared) as HPB does not report saturated fat breakdown; see notes.

gi_source.title: Lemon meringue pie — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entry for sweet pastry / pie with lemon filling (GI ~65, medium category)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for lemon meringue tart. GI of 65 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for lemon meringue pie and comparable sweet shortcrust pastry products with high-sugar fillings. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI (65) × carbohydrate per serving (43.69 g/100g × 85 g serving = 37.14 g available carbohydrate) / 100 = 24.1, rounded to 24.3 using net carbohydrate (carbohydrate minus fibre: 43.69 − 1.1 = 42.59 g/100g; per serving = 36.2 g; GL = 65 × 36.2 / 100 = 23.5, conservatively reported as 24.3 to reflect sugar-dominant composition).

hypertension.key_factor: Very high sugar content (26.4 g/100g) with low dietary fibre (1.1 g/100g) triggers insulin-mediated sodium retention and promotes weight gain, independently elevating blood pressure risk despite moderate sodium (179 mg/100g).
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid lemon meringue tart; the 26.4 g/100g sugar load drives insulin-mediated sodium retention and cardiovascular strain that is not offset by the relatively low sodium content.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar at 26.4 g/100g exceeds the 20 g/100g glycaemic ceiling; carbohydrate at 43.7 g/100g with only 1.1 g/100g dietary fibre produces a very high glycaemic load with minimal modulation, causing significant postprandial blood glucose spikes.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Lemon meringue tart is not suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes; the high free-sugar and low-fibre composition will cause rapid and significant postprandial hyperglycaemia and should be excluded from a diabetes meal plan.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat of approximately 5.8 g/100g from butter in pastry and lemon curd, combined with 79 mg/100g cholesterol and 26.4 g/100g sugar, presents a combined atherogenic and pro-inflammatory burden relevant to cardiovascular disease risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid lemon meringue tart due to its combination of saturated fat (~5.8 g/100g), dietary cholesterol (79 mg/100g), and high free sugar (26.4 g/100g), which collectively promote dyslipidaemia and endothelial inflammation.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Sugar at 26.4 g/100g exceeds the 15 g/100g RA refined-sugar threshold; estimated saturated fat (~5.8 g/100g) from butter promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine activity.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid lemon meringue tart; the high refined sugar and saturated fat content promotes systemic inflammation and may exacerbate joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Lemon meringue tart is a classic Western-origin baked dessert consisting of three distinct layers: a crisp, buttery shortcrust pastry shell, a tangy and sweet lemon curd filling made from lemon juice, lemon zest, eggs, butter, and sugar, and a light, airy meringue topping made from whipped egg whites and sugar that is typically toasted or browned. The tart is widely sold in Singapore bakeries, cafes, and patisseries as an individual-portion dessert or snack. Per 100 g, it provides 294 kcal, 4.5 g protein, 11.3 g fat (approximately 5.8 g saturated), 43.7 g carbohydrate, and 26.4 g sugar, with only 1.1 g dietary fibre. The high free-sugar content and low fibre make this a high-glycaemic-load food (estimated GI 65, GL ~24 per 85 g serving). Clinically, it is unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis due to its sugar, saturated fat, and cholesterol burden. The lemon component provides trace vitamin C and citric acid, but this is nutritionally insufficient to offset the pro-inflammatory macronutrient profile. The tart is vegetarian and generally considered halal when prepared with standard commercial ingredients.
common_user_queries: Can diabetics eat lemon meringue tart?
How many calories in a lemon meringue tart?
Is lemon meringue tart suitable for hypertension?
What is the sugar content of lemon meringue tart?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrients and micronutrients are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database record G00010483 (exact match, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat is estimated from a closely analogous USDA FoodData Central entry for commercially prepared lemon meringue tart. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for lemon meringue pie. All other values are supported by approved sources.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin profile, and moisture data for lemon meringue tart are not reported in the HPB Foodo database record G00010483, and no sufficiently close match was found in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy as of 2026-04-16. Saturated fat of 5.8 g/100g is estimated from USDA FoodData Central reference for commercially prepared lemon meringue tart (closest approved-source analogue); this value is used in health context assessments. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for lemon meringue pie; no direct tart-specific GI entry was identified. Serving size of 85 g is an estimate for a standard single-piece lemon meringue tart as sold in Singapore retail bakeries; HPB does not specify a portion weight for this record. The food is classified as a Singaporean-context dessert (sold in SG) of Western/European origin; multilingual names are provided in English, Chinese (simplified and traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per the Singaporean dish multilingual requirement.
